title: Roasted Saudi Lamb Tart
description: A charred, light meal-prep dish featuring puff pastry filled with mortar‑pestle‑pounded lamb seasoned with Saudi spices, no onion or garlic, perfect for a bento box lunch.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200 degrees Celsius and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

Place lamb cubes in a mortar and pound with a pestle until they are roughly 2 centimeters thick, adding sea salt, pepper, rosemary, cumin, coriander, cardamom, and a splash of lemon juice while mixing.
Drizzle olive oil over the lamb and mix until the meat is evenly coated.
Roll out each puff pastry sheet to a 3 millimeter thickness and cut into 6 by 6 centimeter squares.
Spoon a tablespoon of the seasoned lamb onto the center of each pastry square, leaving a 1 centimeter border around the edges.
Fold the pastry over the lamb to form a half‑moon shape and press the edges together, sealing with a fork.
Place the tarts on the prepared baking sheet and bake for 15 minutes, until the pastry is golden and the lamb is charred at the edges.
Remove the tarts from the oven and let them cool for 5 minutes before cutting them into bite‑sized pieces.
Pack the finished tarts into a bento box with a small side of plain yogurt or a squeeze of fresh lemon for a light meal.
Store any leftovers in the refrigerator and reheat in a toaster oven for 5 minutes before serving.
